head.hi: Prime Minister Churchill to President Roosevelt
head.note: Sent to London; forwarded to Washington, apparently via United States military channels; and forwarded further by the White House Map Room to Roosevelt in North Africa.

opener.dateline: [Cairo?] 21 November 1943.According to Alanbrooke (p. 74), Churchill arrived at Cairo at 2:30 p.m. by plane from Alexandria.

div.p: Former Naval Person to President Roosevelt personal and most secret. No. 506.

list.item: My arrival in Egypt is bound to be known as I shall pass through to see Catroux and others: moreover British Parliament meets on 23rd and my absence must be explained. Unless I hear from you to the contrary I shall allow it to be stated on 22nd that I am in Cairo.
This publicity will be unsupported cover for your movement which I think should not be announced for a few days.
You will be receiving a telegram about military precautionsTelegram 508 of November 21, 1943, post, p. 105. which are excellent.
